message:
debug(codegen): Add extensive debug output to trace test_raise.py crash location

PROBLEM: test_raise.py crashes with exit code 132 (SIGILL) when processing TestContext class

INVESTIGATION:
Added comprehensive debug output throughout genClassDef() to pinpoint exact crash location:
- Class processing start/end
- Test method scanning and skip detection
- State save/restore
- Captured variables
- Field generation
- Init method generation
- Method generation

FINDINGS:
- Crash location: body.genClassMethods() when generating TestContext's 13 test methods
- Previous optimizations (skip hoisting for test methods, build class_names once) work correctly
- All pre-method-generation steps complete successfully
- Crash happens INSIDE genClassMethods, likely when processing test_3118 or test_3611
- These methods have nested functions and local classes which may cause stack overflow

NEXT STEPS:
- Investigate genClassMethods implementation in body/class_methods.zig
- Add method-level debug output to identify which specific method triggers crash
- Check for infinite recursion or excessive stack usage in nested class/function handling

Files changed:
- generators.zig: Added ~50 debug print statements throughout genClassDef()

NOTE: All debug output uses std.debug.print() and should be removed once crash is fixed
